---
title: "Static Assets"
page-layout: full
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

# Bird Media Structure

Bird photos (`img/`) and sounds (`audio/`) are organized into **one
folder per species**, named `{species_id}_{Common_Name}` (spaces
replaced with underscores). This matches how the app resolves assets at
runtime:

``` python
folder_name = f"{species_id}_{common_name.replace(' ', '_')}"
```

App-level layout images (logo, favicon, etc.) live at the top level of
`img/`, *outside* any species folder.

## Images — `app/www/img/`

```         
app/www/img/
├── favicon.png                       # app-level layout assets (top level)
├── BAM-Logo-WhiteText.svg
├── DOWO_Downy_Woodpecker/            # {species_id}_{Common_Name}
│   ├── 1_667108186.jpg               # {index}_{iNaturalist_photo_id}.jpg
│   ├── 1_667108186_metadata.json     # sidecar: <image_stem>_metadata.json
│   ├── 2_666466435.jpg
│   ├── 2_666466435_metadata.json
│   └── ...                           # up to 12 photos per species
└── .../
```

**Naming conventions**

- **Photo:** `{index}_{photo_id}.jpg` — `index` is 1…N in display order;
  `photo_id` is the source iNaturalist photo ID.
- **Metadata sidecar:** the image stem + `_metadata.json` (e.g.
  `1_667108186.jpg` → `1_667108186_metadata.json`). The app pairs each
  image to its metadata by exact filename stem.
- **Per species:** up to **12** photos — **6 male + 6 female** for
  sexually distinguishable species, or **12 unlabelled** where sex can't
  be determined.

**Image metadata fields**

| Field | Type | Description |
|--------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------|
| `common_name` | string | English common name |
| `scientific_name` | string | Scientific name |
| `sex` | string | `"male"`, `"female"`, or `"unknown"` — drives the ♂/♀ badge |
| `life_stage` | string | e.g. `"adult"` |
| `captive` | bool | `false` for wild observations |
| `photo_id` | int | iNaturalist photo ID |
| `attribution` | string | Photographer / rights string for display |
| `license` | string | License code (e.g. `cc-by-nc`, `cc0`) |
| `obs_url` | string | Link to the source iNaturalist observation |
| `obs_date` | string | Observation date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) |
| `recorded_by` | string | iNaturalist user login of the observer |
| `download_url` | string | Source URL the image was fetched from |
| `size_kb` | float | Approximate file size in KB |
| `local_file` | string | The image filename (mirrors the `.jpg` name) |

## Sounds — `app/www/audio/`

```         
app/www/audio/
└── DOWO_Downy_Woodpecker/            # same {species_id}_{Common_Name} convention
    ├── 1_<source_id>.mp3             # {index}_{source_id}.mp3
    ├── 1_<source_id>_metadata.json   # sidecar, keyed by leading index
    └── ...                           # app plays up to 5 clips per species
```

Audio clips are sourced from Xeno-Canto. The app loads up to **5**
`.mp3` files per species (sorted by filename) and pairs each with its
metadata by the leading index of the filename. The player consumes the
following metadata fields: `recordist`, `country`, `date`, `quality`,
`license`, and `xeno_canto_url` (falling back to `obs_url`).
